relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: One hidden theft is narrated as unfaithfulness belonging to the whole community.
Joshua sends another reconnaissance mission toward Ai.
The scouts minimize the city and recommend a small force.
Three thousand go up and flee.
Thirty-six die, and Israel's confidence melts into water.
Joshua and the elders collapse in grief before the ark.
Joshua questions the crossing itself when defeat arrives.
He fears what can be said after Israel turns its back.
His prayer joins communal survival to the reputation of the Divine name.
Joshua is told to rise from lament into investigation.
The hidden act is named as taking, stealing, deceiving, and concealing.
The whole people is treated as contaminated by one person's theft.
The community prepares for exposure of what was hidden.
The search narrows from tribe to clan to household to person.
The announced penalty reaches the accused and everything belonging to him.
Judah is selected.
The selection closes around Zerah and Zabdi.
Achan is finally taken.
Joshua addresses him as son while demanding confession.
Achan admits the act.
Seeing becomes coveting, coveting becomes taking, and the objects disappear beneath the tent.
Messengers uncover the hidden goods exactly where he said.
The private treasure is poured out before the whole assembly.
Achan's children, animals, tent, and possessions are carried with him to the valley.
The community answers one man's theft by killing and burning a household.
A heap of stones closes the scene and names the valley Trouble.

contemplative_note: Joshua 7 recognizes that concealed wrongdoing can damage a community, but it answers that insight with collective killing. The ethical inheritance must stop before the stones: expose hidden harm, establish evidence, hold the responsible person accountable, and protect every child, dependent, animal, and relative from inherited punishment.
